opps.title: AF12--Intent to Sole Source    Scientific Journal Supplement Publication Service -  Academic Emergency Medicine
opps.description:
The Department of Veterans Affairs, VA Boston Healthcare System, Center for Information Dissemination and Education Resources (CIDER) intends to negotiate on a sole source basis, pursuant to FAR 6.302-1, with the Society for Academic Emergency Medicine (SAEM) for scientific journal supplement publication services. 

CIDER requires services to produce a special journal supplement of peer-reviewed manuscripts in the Academic Emergency Medicine (AEM) Journal of Society for Academic Emergency Medicine (SAEM). The manuscripts written by VA Health Service Research and Development Service (HSR&D) and invited investigators/respondents will be focused on the  state of the art of emergency care in the VA healthcare system.  AEM/SAEM is uniquely qualified due to its emphasis on clinical trials, observational cohort studies, and economic or policy research, all of which are a part of health services research; its ranking as a top journal in the field; and its target audience and membership cohort of academic emergency physicians to produce a special journal supplement.
 
This notice of intent is not a request for competitive proposals; however, any responsible source who believes it is capable of meeting the requirement may submit a capability statement to the contracting office no later than Wednesday, February 23, 2022, 3:00 PM, EST.  Interest/capability statements may be sent to Shannon Hukriede at Shannon.Hukriede@va.gov. No telephone responses will be accepted. A determination not to compete the proposed requirement based upon the responses to this notice is solely within the discretion of the Government.

opps.title: Access Required to Online Real Estate Database Services
opps.description:
This is a combined synopsis/solicitation for commercial items/services prepared in accordance with the format in Federal Acquisition Regulations (FAR) subpart 12.6, “Streamlined Procedures for Evaluation and Solicitation for Commercial Items or Services”, and FAR Part 13 Simplified Acquisition Procedures, as supplemented with additional information included in this notice. This announcement constitutes the only solicitation; quotes are being requested. This solicitation number is 2043FY-26-Q-00038 and is issued as a Request for Quote (RFQ). 

The Internal Revenue Service (IRS) is seeking commercial real estate database subscription services to support the Large Business and International (LB&I) Division's Engineering Program.

The LB&I Division’s Engineering Program provides technical support on engineering and valuation issues involving large and complex business tax returns. Specialists perform work involving commercial real estate appraisal, machinery and equipment valuation, cost segregation studies, and research credit claims.

To support these activities, the Government requires access to a commercial real estate information platform that provides nationwide current and historical market data. The platform must provide comprehensive commercial real estate information, including ownership records, sales transactions, lease information, market trends, and related market analytics necessary to evaluate commercial properties and support tax administration activities. The Government intends to award a contract to the responsible offeror whose quotation conforms to the solicitation requirements and offers the lowest evaluated price. This requirement is unrestricted for full and open competition.

The Statement of Work and all applicable solicitation documents are included as attachments to this notice. Sources who believe they can provide the products/services included in the Statement of Work (SOW) shall submit a price quote via email

opps.title: Master Synch
opps.description:
The National Audio-Visual Conservation Center (NAVCC) unit of the Library of Congress (LOC) is an audiovisual preservation and digitization facility located on the Packard Campus in Culpeper, VA.

The Video Lab at the NAVCC is tasked with the digitization of multiple tape and disc formats of analog and digital video. It was designed and built to substantially emulate a video production facility and uses an Evertz Master Sync Generator system. This system provides crucial reference video test signals for the video lab. It allows the VTR’s (video tape recorder) to lock on to a reference signal which provides the most optimal conditions for error free digitization of analog video and audio into the digital signal which is then used by the encoders to create archival files. The current system is obsolete and has been damaged beyond repair in certain areas such as the GPS antenna and cabling, which has suffered rodent damage.

The Library needs a contractor that will provide system hardware, installation and documentation for the replacement Master Sync system in the Video Lab at NAVCC.
opps.summary:
Background

The NAVCC is responsible for acquiring, preserving, and providing access to the world’s largest collection of films, television and radio broadcasts, videos, and sound recordings. 

The Video Lab at NAVCC is tasked with digitizing multiple tape and disc formats of analog and digital video using an Evertz Master Sync Generator system, which is currently obsolete and damaged beyond repair.

opps.title: Managed Attribution Systems (Chameleon)
opps.description:
The Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA) Cyber Support Section (ODV) needs to continue to modernize and streamline DEA’s Internet-based investigative resources with an integrated managed attribution and evidence collection system. Deployment of these capabilities, across the entire DEA will equip agents and analysts with standardized technical tools necessary to safely and effectively conduct online research and collect and preserve evidence.

Conducting Internet-based investigations and research browsing has multiplied over the past several years. With the advent of Drug Trafficking Organizations (DTOs) relying heavily upon Internet-based applications and Dark Web sites to facilitate their criminal enterprise, it is anticipated that in the future, electronic communications will be the preferred and the exclusive means of communication. The threat landscape described creates opportunities for law enforcement to exploit illicit Dark Web market sites utilized by DTOs and dismantle their operations.

opps.description: The U.S. Department of Transportation is establishing a department-wide, multiple-award Blanket Purchase Agreement to modernize its IT infrastructure under the "1DOT" initiative, shifting from siloed legacy systems toward shared, enterprise-level digital platforms. This flexible acquisition vehicle provides specialized software engineering, agile development, cloud-native architecture, and AI/ML solutions to support both mission-specific and department-wide applications. To meet socioeconomic goals while maintaining high-level technical capacity, the requirement utilizes a dual-pool structure that defaults to small business set-asides for most tasks while reserving complex, cross-modal projects for an unrestricted competition pool. Ultimately, this vehicle enables the DOT to rapidly deploy multidisciplinary agile teams to deliver secure, scalable, and human-centered digital services across all its operating administrations. This opportunity supersedes SWES 2.0. To be added to the distribution list for this opportunity, please contact an acquisition member listed on this forecast item. The Draft RFP will be published at GSA eBuy! (https://www.ebuy.gsa.gov/ebuy/).

This is a notice of intent to solicit and award a sole source contract in accordance with the acquisition of commercial products and commercial services under the simplified acquisition threshold in accordance with Revolutionary FAR Overhaul (RFO) Subpart 12.102, Restricting Competition.  

The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) Office of Procurement Operations (OPO) on behalf of the DHS Science and Technology Directorate (S&T) hereby issues this notice of intent to award a sole source contract to Bloomberg Industry Group, Inc, 1801 South Bell Street Arlington, VA 22202.

 Background

In support of the S&T mission, many divisions need access to certain scientific journals and databases to remain at the forefront of their research.  Bloomberg Government is a premier platform for federal policy, budget, and legislative intelligence. The proposed purchase will enable DHS S&T staff to access real-time government data, track legislative developments, and conduct policy analysis, thereby enhancing operational effectiveness and strategic decision-making. The acquisition is expected to streamline research, improve situational awareness, and support mission-critical activities.  S&T will be procure five  (5) subscriptions for (5) users.

Bloomberg Government (BGOV) is a unique, proprietary platform that provides comprehensive, real-time federal legislative, regulatory, and budgetary intelligence specifically tailored for government affairs professionals. No other commercially available product offers the same breadth and depth of integrated data, advanced analytics, real-time alerts, and customizable dashboards in a single solution. BGOV’s exclusive aggregation of congressional activity, appropriations tracking, policy analysis, and federal contract intelligence is essential for DHS S&T’s mission to monitor and respond to rapidly evolving legislative and regulatory developments. rex_analysis.what_they_need: Department of Education Institute of Education Sciences (IES) needs a contractor to evaluate the effectiveness of Workforce Pell Grants — a federal program allowing Pell Grant funding for short-term workforce-focused training. Evaluation assesses program design, implementation, and outcomes using rigorous research methodology.
rex_analysis.rex_take: BLN24 is stronger here than the initial score suggests. IRS SB/SE BOD Leadership Initiative ($2.7M via Clarity24) is a DIRECT workforce development / human capital reference — leadership development, culture assessment for 60+ IRS executives, organizational transformation, capacity building. That directly maps to evaluating workforce training programs. EDARMST $2.5M is same agency (IES). Fors Marsh JV covers evaluation methodology via DHRA $59M behavioral research. Teaming play: BLN24/Clarity24 leads workforce dev + data analytics, Fors Marsh JV leads evaluation design. MRAS response is low-effort. Respond now.
rex_analysis.watch_notes:
Respond via Qualtrics form: https://feedback.gsa.gov/jfe/form/SV_8bPc93UqNaEsfIi
Highlight: IRS SB/SE Leadership Initiative (Clarity24 $2.7M) for workforce dev, EDARMST $2.5M for same-agency IES past performance, Fors Marsh JV for evaluation methodology.

opps.description:
THIS IS A REQUEST FOR INFORMATION ONLY

The U.S. Department of Homeland Security (DHS), Office of Procurement Operations (OPO) issues this Request for Information (RFI) on behalf of the DHS Science and Technology Directorate (S&T).

The Department of Homeland Security (DHS), Directorate of Science and Technology (S&T) Office Science and Engineering (OSE) is conducting market research to determine the capabilities of industry of digital forensics services to support verification and validation of assorted identity documents.  These could include but not be limited to the following capabilities:

•   Expert consulting, integration, and development services to facilitate the examination, creation, testing, and replication of integrated circuit systems or digital security elements contained within physical secure identity documents e.g. (cryptographic signatures in barcodes) including passports, driver’s licenses, ID cards, vital records, etc.

•   Expert consulting, integration, and development services to facilitate the examination, creation, testing, and replication of Verifiable Digital Credential (VDC) versions of physical secure identity documents (e.g. mobile driver’s licenses, digital travel credentials) including passports, driver’s licenses, ID cards, etc.

The purpose of this RFI is to collect information from qualified and interested sources with the capability and experience in providing services identified in the RFI (see attachment).

rex_analysis.what_they_need: Department of Education Institute of Education Sciences (IES) needs a contractor to evaluate the effectiveness of Workforce Pell Grants — a federal program allowing Pell Grant funding for short-term workforce-focused training. Evaluation assesses program design, implementation, and outcomes using rigorous research methodology.
rex_analysis.rex_take: BLN24 is stronger here than the initial score suggests. IRS SB/SE BOD Leadership Initiative ($2.7M via Clarity24) is a DIRECT workforce development / human capital reference — leadership development, culture assessment for 60+ IRS executives, organizational transformation, capacity building. That directly maps to evaluating workforce training programs. EDARMST $2.5M is same agency (IES). Fors Marsh JV covers evaluation methodology via DHRA $59M behavioral research. Teaming play: BLN24/Clarity24 leads workforce dev + data analytics, Fors Marsh JV leads evaluation design. MRAS response is low-effort. Respond now.

Highlight: IRS SB/SE Leadership Initiative (Clarity24 $2.7M) for workforce dev, EDARMST $2.5M for same-agency IES past performance, Fors Marsh JV for evaluation methodology.

opps.title: Depot Operations Support
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Custom Computer Programming (web/software — MBDA/Census lane)
opps.description:
The United States Department of Agriculture's (USDA) Client Experience Center (CEC) is accountable for managing the Information Technology (IT) infrastructure and service delivery to USDA. 

The USDA Enterprise Depot's primary mission is to provide centralized and standardized device deployment and excessing services to over 100,000 USDA customers across the United States and its territories. 

The USDA Enterprise Depot is a hybrid facility that performs receiving, storage, inventory management, asset management, device (laptop, desktop, and tablet) preparation, OS imaging/deployment, kitting, pick and ship, returns processing, shipping, and excessing of IT equipment such as laptops, desktops, tablets, mobile printers, monitors, cords, keyboards, flash drives, cable locks, and mice. The USDA Enterprise Depot is partnered and integrated with 3rd party vendors to assist with overflow storage and shipping services.

opps.title: Electronic Security Consulting Services
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Custom Computer Programming (web/software — MBDA/Census lane)
opps.description:
Provide services in accordance with the attached Statement of Work. The primary goal of establishing this contract is for electronic security consulting services to support the procurement and design phases of the U.S. Marshals Service (USMS) electronic security system project for Courthouses in Hartford, CT, and Chattanooga, TN.

Please read the attached documents for information on the scope of work and additional information included. The consulting services outlined focus on supporting GSA through scope development, design review, procurement, and initial security contractor onboarding for new electronic security systems (Access Control, Video Surveillance, Intrusion Detection, and Intercom/Emergency Communication). Services will primarily be performed remotely, with necessary on-site activities at Washington, D.C., Atlanta, GA, Hartford, CT, and Chattanooga, TN. The estimated duration for these services is 2-years from the commencement date, with implementation support projected from August 3, 2026, to August 3, 2028.

The Government intends to award an order for services in accordance with FAR 8.4 (GSA Class Deviation RFO-2025-08) on the basis of best value to the Government. All evaluation factors other than cost or price, when combined, are approximately equal to price. 

The technical factors that will be evaluated are the offerors Past Experience and Key Personnel, and will be of equal weight when evaluating the proposals.

Past Experience

The Offeror shall demonstrate past experience designing, installing, integrating, testing, and commissioning electronic security systems for facilities of similar size, scope, and complexity. Relevant systems include access control, video surveillance, intrusion detection, duress alarms, intercom systems, alarm monitoring, and related security infrastructure.

The Offeror shall submit two project examples completed within the past five years. These examples shall involve federal facilities or courthouses. Project exampl

opps.description:
The purpose of this update is to inform industry partners and interested vendors of a schedule change regarding the release of the upcoming solicitation for FS3i.  The issuance of the formal solicitation has been delayed. The Government now anticipates releasing the solicitation later than anticipated but still within the current calendar year.  This schedule adjustment is necessary to accommodate planned minor modifications to the Performance Work Statement (PWS), as well as targeted revisions to Section L (Instructions, Conditions, and Notices to Offerors) and Section M (Evaluation Factors for Award).


The Government is implementing these revisions to directly improve the overall solicitation process. By refining the PWS and updating Sections L and M, the Government aims to:

Provide greater clarity and precision regarding the mission requirements.

Ensure proposal instructions are straightforward and unambiguous.

Streamline the evaluation criteria to facilitate a more efficient, transparent, and fair assessment of all proposals.


Interested parties are advised to continue monitoring SAM.gov for future updates, draft documents, and the eventual release of the official Solicitation. No further action is required from industry at this time.

			Integrated layered data sources, including ADS-B and satellite AIS for aircraft and maritime detection, supported by scalable machine learning capabilities

opps.title: AN/TMQ-53 Tactical Meteorological Observing System (TMOS) Contractor Logistics Support (CLS)
opps.description:
This RFP has been Amended with the following changes. Due date extended to 20 July 2026; added the Questions and Answers, PWS Rev03, A022 CDRL attachment, Section L Rev02, and Solicitation Amendment 0001. All other terms, conditions, and requirements remain unchanged.

The Air Force Life Cycle Management Center (AFLCMC), Aerospace Management Systems Division, Weather Systems Branch (AFLCMC/ESAK), Hanscom AFB, MA 01731, is issuing a formal Request for Proposal (RFP) to provide Tactical Meteorological Observing System (TMOS) Contractor Logistics Support (CLS) for sustainment efforts supporting TMQ-53 systems from FY26 to FY31.

The contractor shall provide worldwide Contractor Logistics Support (CLS) to sustain currently fielded TMQ-53 systems in their baseline configuration. This effort encompasses comprehensive program management, logistics support (including helpdesk, maintenance, and spares/repair management), Operational, Safety, Suitability, and Effectiveness (OSS&E) impact analysis, test and evaluation, Configuration Control Board (CCB) support, and obsolescence and systems engineering. Additionally, the contractor is responsible for tracking and reporting critical system performance metrics (such as System Availability, Mission Capable, and MTBF rates), managing program data (including GFM-A, materials, shipping, and travel), and providing computer resource support, Engineering Change Proposals (ECPs), cybersecurity, technical manual maintenance, and system upgrades due to technical refresh. While worldwide support is required, there is no requirement for contractor travel to overseas locations.

This proposed effort is a total small business set-aside. The Government has determined, IAW FAR Subpart 19.103, that the NAICS classification of 541990 - All Other Professional, Scientific, and Technical Services best describes the principal nature of the product and service being acquired and represents the greatest percentage of the contract price. This classif

opps.title: Google Workspace Enterprise Plus
opps.description:
This procurement, supporting the Office of the Chief Information Officer (OCIO), is for the use of Google Workspace Enterprise Plus Apps to support CISAâ€™s IT Operations performance functions. The Enterprise Plus apps are a cloud-based browser with built-in controls that provide encryption and verification which enables work from anywhere and eliminates the need for VPNs. The apps have the ability to operate on a global scale while protecting information from phishing, malware, ransomware, and supply chain attacks. And the apps have secure endpoints that do not require patching and strong account takeover protections.

The software will be used to analyze CISA datasets to enhance leadership awareness of trends and organizational performance. The software procured must be capable of conducting real- time analysis; be relatively easy to use; allow data to flow easily into and out of the software; have forecasting capabilities that can predict values based on trends in the dataset; utilize user- friendly dashboards to format visualizations; and support professional reporting formats and data visualizations suitable for executive-level presentations.

 The contractor shall provide the CISA Office of the Chief Information Officer (OCIO) with the following: 
1. Google Workspace Enterprise Plus; New â€“ Quantity: 1,300, 
2. Google Workspace Enterprise Plus; Archived â€“ Quantity: 1,300, 
3. G Suite Support Base Fee; - Quantity: 1, 
4. G Suite Support Variable Fee; - Quantity: 1,
5. Cloud Platform â€“ Support G Suite; Quantity: 50,000

Broadnet
UEI: UDTNUZMDNFC8
2679 W. Main Street
Suite 757
Littleton, CO 80120

This is done under authority of 41 U.S.C. 3304(a)(1), as implemented by FAR 6.302-1: Only one source and no other supplies or services will satisfy agency requirements.

While this synopsis does not constitute a solicitation, interested vendors may identify their interest and capability to satisfy the requirement.

No phone calls. Please indicate  Response to Internet Townhall Services   NWI  in subject line of e-mail.


Such documentation will be reviewed solely for the purpose of determining whether or not to conduct the procurement on a competitive basis. A determination not to compete this requirement, based upon responses to this notice, is solely within the discretion of the Government under authorities cited above.

It is the Offeror's responsibility to ensure proposals are complete and submitted early enough to be received by the GSA POC before the deadline, and to confirm receipt before the deadline. Proposals or portions of proposals which are received by the GSA POC later than the time noted within will not be accepted, reviewed, or considered.

NOTE: The maximum file size is 25 MB, therefore contractors/offerors need to plan accordingly.

opps.title: Collaborative Autonomy Software Framework Development
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Custom Computer Programming (web/software — MBDA/Census lane)
opps.description:
The Air Force Life Cycle Management Center (AFLCMC/WISJ) is conducting market research to identify sources capable of serving as the primary developer for an advanced, Government-owned collaborative autonomy software framework. This open-architecture software stack is designed to enable collaborative, autonomous capabilities across a variety of crewed and uncrewed systems. The baseline for this framework is an evolution of complex, multi-agent swarming autonomy architectures previously developed under Air Force Research Lab (AFRL) initiatives.
The Government’s objective is to evaluate industry capability to perform continuous software development, applied research, testing, and maturation of this framework in accordance with the DoDI 5000.87 Software Acquisition Pathway. The scope requires the contractor to develop and mature the core software logic, integrate the software onto diverse platforms, integrate third-party vendor capabilities into the open framework, perform applied research on future autonomy behaviors, and develop ground station integration.

opps.description:
Enclosed is a Request for Quotations (RFQ)for Public Opinion Research Surveys.

The U.S. Government intends to award a contract to the responsible company submitting an acceptable offer at the lowest price.  We intend to award a contract based on initial quotations, without holding discussions, although we may hold discussions with companies in the competitive range if there is a need to do so. We may also request clarification or best and final offers prior to award selection.

FOR QUESTIONS

Any and all questions regarding this solicitation must be submitted in writing, in English, on or before the deadline. Questions must be sent to AmmanRFQQuestions@state.gov. The Subject Line of your email should read Question(s) re: 19J01026Q0014. The deadline is July 20, 2026.  The U.S. Embassy will publish all questions and answers as soon as possible after the deadline above.



HOW TO SUBMIT

To submit an offer or quotation, you must follow the instructions in Section 3 of the Solicitation; complete the required sections of the attached document(s); and submit a comprehensive offer package in a single email on or before the due date, according to the instructions below. Failure to follow any of the instructions will disqualify your proposal from being reviewed. Under no circumstances will late or incomplete packages be reviewed.

Failure to complete or submit a required document or element will result in the offer being determined incomplete and will result in disqualification. You should carefully review the requirements in the full Solicitation as well as all supporting materials, to include this cover letter. Required materials typically include:

	Section I, Pricing
	Section 5 Representations and Certifications
	Additional information as required in Section 3
	Proof of SAM Registration

opps.description: The Department of Veterans Affairs, Office of Research and Development intends to negotiate on a sole source basis, pursuant to FAR 6.302-1, with Mary Ann Liebert, Inc. for Services   Health Equity Special Collection. The VA Office of Research and Development (ORD) requires services to publish a special collection of articles documenting its efforts and progress in the area of diversity, equity, and inclusion in a well-established and well-respected academic journal specializing in the area of health equity. The journal Health Equity, published by Mary Ann Liebert Inc., is specifically and exclusively focused on the topic of health equity, and is recognized as a leading journal worldwide in this area. This notice of intent is not a request for competitive proposals; however, any responsible source who believes it is capable of meeting the requirement may submit a capability statement to the contracting office no later than Wednesday, January 12, 2022, 5:00 PM, EST.  Interest/capability statements may be sent to Shannon Hukriede at Shannon.Hukriede@va.gov. No telephone responses will be accepted. A determination not to compete the proposed requirement based upon the responses to this notice is solely within the discretion of the Government.

opps.title: Notification of Availability of Software Framework, Warped Alloy, for Software Defined Radio (SDR) Signal Chain
opps.searchLabel: NAICS: Custom Computer Programming (web/software — MBDA/Census lane)
opps.description:
The Department of the Army is pleased to announce the availability of a cutting-edge software framework designed to enhance the capabilities of Software Defined Radio (SDR) systems. This framework enables the SDR signal chain to dynamically adjust its capabilities based on the sensed RF environment and to respond at the protocol level in real-time.

The Department of the Army is releasing this software framework to foster collaboration with industry partners and encourage the integration of this technology into their SDR products. The intent is to enable greater flexibility in conducting radio frequency operations, allowing industry partners to leverage this framework to enhance their SDR systems' adaptability and operational effectiveness. By providing this framework, we aim to advance the state of SDR technology and promote innovation in the field of radio frequency operations.

Key Features of the Framework:


	Rapid Reconfiguration: The framework allows for swift reconfiguration of the RF signal processing chain modules within the SDR’s field programmable gate array (FPGA), ensuring adaptability to varying operational requirements.
	FPGA-Based Physical Layer (PHY) and Media Access Control (MAC): The framework enables the rapid development and implementation of a fully-customized PHY and MAC layer that supports various signals and waveforms, providing a comprehensive solution for SDR applications.


Elements Available for Industry:


	FPGA Top-Level Framework: This top-level hardware description language (HDL) framework resides within the SDR FPGA, facilitating seamless communication between the General-Purpose Processor (GPP) and the signal modules.
	Application Programming Interface (API): A user-friendly, common C-based API is provided, allowing developers to create SDR-agnostic applications that can manipulate the programmed signal FPGA modules effectively.
	Transport Interface (TI): The TI consists of dynamic or static C libraries utilized during a

opps.title: Illegal Passing of School Bus Violation Enforcement in the Court Sys & Model Law
opps.description:
As stated in the recently completed State Laws Digest for Illegal Passing of School Buses, the term "illegal passing" is a prevalent concept; however, the report illustrated its lack of universality. Variations in state laws dictate that passing a bus from the front on a divided highway may be illegal in some states but permissible under specific roadway conditions in others. What constitutes illegal behavior in one jurisdiction may not be prohibited in a neighboring one. Given the high mobility of the U.S. population, the proximity of urban centers to multiple jurisdictions, and the significant disparities in the content and language of applicable Vehicle and Traffic Laws (VTL), confusion regarding the legality of actions surrounding a school bus is not unexpected.

The lack of clarity in drafting illegal passing provisions further compromises the ability of the typical motorist to understand these VTL requirements. Some jurisdictions have redrafted their amended provisions to include, among other things, the use of cameras. Thus, their updates present a mostly coherent, integrated statement of the law. Other jurisdictions have provisions that appear essentially sequential in design. New or changed provisions simply appear at the end of the existing VTL section, even if they logically relate to a topic much earlier in the material. This type of statutory construction is difficult to follow and can be misleading.

As recommended in the state laws digest, and in anticipation of future legislative interest, this project will focus more deeply on current illegal passing laws and develop a draft model state law to address incidents of illegal passing of stopped school buses. This report will also investigate the potential benefits and feasibility of the citation being deemed a moving violation.

This project will review the current research and practice of enforcement of the law against illegal passing of school buses. This project will provide if a model law would be n
opps.summary:
Background
 The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) is issuing this solicitation to address the growing concern for student safety on and around school buses. The project aims to develop a model law for the illegal passing of school buses, which varies significantly across jurisdictions, leading to confusion and inconsistent enforcement. The initiative is prompted by alarming statistics indicating that illegal passing incidents result in serious injuries and fatalities among children.

ExLibris, is the only source that can provide the licenses due to the proprietary nature of the licences and data., no other company was found that has authorized approval

This notice of intent is not a request for competitive proposals and no solicitation document exists for the requirement.
Sources interested in responding to this notice are required to submit a capability statement that includes technical capabilities and cost information, in sufficient detail and with convincing evidence that clearly demonstrates the capability to provide the licenses and proof as being an authorized distributor of the product.  Capability statements shall not exceed 6 (8.5 x 11 inch) pages using a font size no smaller than 10-point.  All capability statements received by the due date of this notice will be considered by the Government. A request for documentation or additional information or submissions that only ask questions will not be considered as an affirmative response. A determination by the Government not to compete based on responses to this notice is solely within the discretion of the Government.
Information received will be considered solely for the purpose of determining whether to conduct a competitive procurement or to proceed with a sole source purchase order.

Capability statements shall be submitted by e-mail ONLY as a Microsoft Word or Adobe PDF attachment to the following addres
